title: Viburnum grandifolium
intro: Viburnum grandifolium, also known as bigleaf viburnum, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, valued for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Viburnum grandifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Viburnum grandifolium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and digestive issues.
